codeReview.summary: The fix addresses both prior blockers. match-performance now preserves latest_riot_* and latest_db_* head markers monotonically when older stale rows are reprocessed, and stale detection now reads per-match freshness state from player_sync_metadata.notes plus row/cache comparisons so one refreshed row no longer hides other stale recent rows for the same player.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The web-feature-worker skill still frames browser verification as a normal required step even for server/data-freshness fixes like this one, so workers must record an avoidable deviation when the feature is primarily API/database logic.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Feature player-sync-metadata-correctness-fix in mission  eatures.json uses web-feature-worker; the handoff's skillFeedback.followedProcedure is false solely because browser verification was skipped for this non-UI fix, and web-feature-worker/SKILL.md step 6 is written around UI verification.

summary: Reviewed the prior fail report, fix handoff, transcript skeleton, skill file, and commit cc31b0e. The fix passes: latest Riot/DB head metadata now stays monotonic during stale-row reprocessing, and stale derived-row detection is match-specific rather than player-wide, so the prior blocking scrutiny findings for leagueback-player-sync-metadata are addressed.
